pattern: A depth-first walk that carries the chain of ancestor values down each branch emits one formatted path string whenever it reaches a node with no children.
hints: Each answer is the sequence of values from the root down to a node that has neither a left nor a right child.
Recurse downward while remembering the values seen so far on the current branch.
When a node has no children, join the remembered values with the arrow separator and record that string; otherwise recurse into each existing child with the value appended.

edge_inputs.description: single node is one path of length one
branch with a missing child so one leaf hangs off the left
negative values must appear verbatim in the path
left-skewed chain yields a single long path
perfect tree of depth three has four leaf paths
internal node with two children plus a sibling leaf

stress.description: random level-order tree with holes so paths vary in length
stress.note: first element non-null so a root always exists
